events.text:
{
  "decision": "fail",
  "risk": "high",
  "work_stage": "in-progress",
  "component_conditions": ["installed-defective", "non-compliant"],
  "percent_complete": 50,
  "value": 0,
  "sound_source": "",
  "confidence": 0.95,
  "event_time": 0,
  "rate": 0,
  "order": [],
  "workable": [],
  "findings": [
    "Belt mistracking due to off-center loading and idler skew creates pinch points and potential for belt edge damage.",
    "Mistracking can cause material spillage, leading to slip/trip hazards and potential for fire from friction.",
    "Uncontrolled belt movement exposes workers to entanglement hazards with rotating parts, violating OSHA 1910.212(a)(1) machine guarding requirements."
  ],
  "actions": [
    "Immediately stop conveyor operation and lockout/tagout energy sources.",
    "Realign loading chute to center material on belt and adjust idlers to correct tracking per CEMA standards.",
    "Inspect and replace damaged belt edges or idlers before restart.",
    "Verify all guards are in place and secure before returning to service."
  ],
  "rationale": "Belt mistracking is a serious safety hazard that can lead to entanglement, fire, and material spillage. OSHA 1910.212 requires guarding of moving parts, and CEMA standards mandate proper belt alignment. The condition is non-compliant and defective, requiring immediate correction.",
  "references": [
    "OSHA 1910.212(a)(1)",
    "CEMA Belt Conveyors for Bulk Materials, Chapter on Belt Training and Idlers"
  ]
}
